title: Mathematics Honours

prerequisites.text: (i) 65% or higher for MAM3000W (or equivalent at another institution); and (ii) the average of the four marks for MAM1000W, MAM1019H, MAM2000W and MAM3000W (or equivalents) should be 65% or higher. Acceptance subject to individual approval by the Head of Department. Students are not allowed to repeat this course. Students are expected to complete the programme in one year.

outline: This course provides an introduction to some topics basic to a professional mathematician. Students complete a mathematics project, at least three of four core modules in Algebra, Analysis, Differential Geometry and Topology, and other modules for a total of at least 160 credits. Most modules are 20 credits; the project (thesis and two seminars) is 40 credits. Typical additional module offerings include a selection from: Algebraic Geometry, Category Theory, Computational Complexity, Cryptology, Differential Topology, Functional Analysis, Graph Theory, Homological Algebra, Lie Algebras, Measure Theory, Number Theory, Operator Theory, Partial Differential Equations, and Theory of Hamiltonian Groups. Students may also pursue approved reading modules.
assessment: Project counts 25% of the final mark and must be passed (50%). On average, the examination counts at least 50% of the balance. Three core modules together count 37.5% of the final mark. The remaining 37.5% is calculated using the student's best marks in other modules. Coursework and project codes must each be passed separately for the award of the degree.
